rows.notes: All extractors agreed on description, type, and empty amounts; visually confirmed as a clear header.

rows.description: Gwinier Rect. pro dim. an. ad h. Annuæ Rec. Sept. 29. 1792
rows.notes: Monetary amounts agreed by both extractors and visually confirmed. `gemini-flash`'s description is a much better visual match to the ledger text, especially the date ('Sept. 29. 1792') and the word 'Annuæ', compared to `gpt-5-mini`'s transcription.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of Gwinear received at Michaelmas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly payment from the rectory of Gwinear received at Michaelmas, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts agreed by both extractors and visually confirmed. `gemini-flash`'s description is a much better visual match to the ledger text, clearly showing 'Hoddington' and 'frument', whereas `gpt-5-mini` misread them as 'Widdrington' and 'present'.
rows.english_description: Payment from the Hoddington rectory received in grain (frument).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from the Hoddington rectory received in grain, amounting to £114 17s 6d.

rows.notes: Monetary amounts agreed by both extractors and visually confirmed. `gemini-flash`'s description is a better visual match; `gpt-5-mini` misread 'gall.' as 'salt.' and 'apringh.' as '&c ?'. The notes from `gpt-5-mini` correctly identified the ambiguity, but `gemini-flash`'s transcription was more accurate in this instance.
rows.english_description: Portion of the Hoddington rectory payment received in capons and poultry.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Portion of the Hoddington rectory payment received in capons and poultry, amounting to £2 15s 0d.

rows.notes: All extractors agreed on description, type, and monetary amounts; visually confirmed as a clear total. Arithmetic check confirms the sum of previous entries.
All extractors agreed on description, type, and empty amounts; visually confirmed as a clear header.

Empty amounts and header type agreed by both. `gpt-5-mini` correctly noted ambiguity in the description, while `gemini-flash` called it 'clear'. However, after visual verification, `gemini-flash`'s specific transcription 'frumentarii' (ending in -ii) is a slightly better visual match than 'frumentarie'. 'parce' vs 'parve' remains ambiguous but `gemini-flash`'s overall text is marginally better.

rows.description: Dna Heywood d. fert annunc. 1793 Rec. July 6th 93
rows.notes: Monetary amounts agreed by both extractors and visually confirmed. `gemini-flash`'s description is a significantly superior visual match to the ledger text, especially 'Dna Heywood d. fert annunc.' and the precise date 'July 6th 93'. `gpt-5-mini`'s description was largely inaccurate, despite its note about ambiguity.
rows.english_description: Payment from Mrs Heywood for the Lady Day (Annunciation) grain rent, recorded 6 July 1793.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Mrs Heywood for the Lady Day (Annunciation) grain rent, recorded 6 July 1793, amounting to £1 1s 5d.

rows.description: B. Gardiner's Herten(?) P. up to Z.X. 1793
rows.notes: Monetary amounts agreed by both extractors and visually confirmed. Both extractors correctly identified ambiguity in the description. `gemini-flash`'s transcription of 'Herten(?)' and 'Z.X.' appears to be a slightly better visual interpretation of the faded and abbreviated text. The inclusion of the apostrophe in 'Gardiner's' by `gemini-flash` is also correct.
rows.english_description: Payment from B. Gardiner relating to grain rents; exact description and period uncertain.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from B. Gardiner relating to grain rents (description and period uncertain), amounting to £0 6s 8d.

rows.notes: All extractors agreed on description, type, and monetary amounts; visually confirmed as a clear total. Arithmetic check confirms the sum of previous entries.
